title: Cryptomorphism: valuated quotients as functions on rank-k flats (Prop. essential-relations)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The entire construction of the involution rests on the asserted but unstated
**Proposition (prop:essential-relations)**: a corank/rank-$k$ valuated matroid quotient
$\theta$ of the trivial valuation on $M$ is equivalent data to a function on the
**rank-$k$ flats** of $M$ satisfying a specific finite list of *essential* relations.

What must be proved:
1. The correct indexing of the rank-$k$ Plücker coordinates of a quotient by rank-$k$ flats of $M$
   (matroid analog of: Plücker coordinates of $[W]\in\Gr(k,\mathbb{F}^r)$ ↔ $k$-dim coordinate subspaces).
2. An **explicit** statement of the essential tropical Plücker / incidence–Plücker relations on these
   flat-indexed coordinates (the problem never writes them down).
3. A proof that this essential system is **equivalent** to the full tropical Plücker + incidence
   system restricted to quotients of the trivial valuation on $M$, i.e. that all non-essential
   relations are implied.

Without (1)–(3) the definition $\theta'(F):=\theta(F^\perp)$ is not even type-correct.
This is the deepest gap and is exactly the content deferred to \cite{wang26modular}.

Tropical Plücker relations are indexed by $(S,T)$ with $|S|=k-1$, $|T|=k+1$ and require
$\min_{a\in T\setminus S}\{\mu(T-a)+\mu(S+a)\}$ to attain its min twice. Recasting these on
rank-$k$ flats should use modular pairs of flats of ranks $k-1$ and $k+1$; the essential relations
are presumably the 3-term relations from such modular pairs. Prove redundancy of the rest via an
exchange / local-to-global argument on the geometric lattice.
